{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Move from a single-resolution block stack to a small two-stage residual CNN with a downsampling transition. That is the next clean capacity gain for this workload while keeping the same optimizer and training budget.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS * CHANNEL_MULT
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            *[
                ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM)
                for _ in range(max(1, DEPTH))
            ],
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","new":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        wide_channels = channels * CHANNEL_MULT
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.stage1 = nn.Sequential(
            *[
                ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM)
                for _ in range(max(1, DEPTH))
            ]
        )
        transition_layers: list[nn.Module] = [
            nn.Conv2d(channels, wide_channels, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias)
        ]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            transition_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(wide_channels))
        transition_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.transition = nn.Sequential(*transition_layers)
        self.stage2 = nn.Sequential(
            *[
                ResidualBlock(wide_channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM)
                for _ in range(max(1, DEPTH))
            ]
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(wide_channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.stage1(x)
        x = self.transition(x)
        x = self.stage2(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Increase capacity where it is cheapest by widening the second residual stage and the classifier head, while keeping the optimizer and schedule fixed. This is a low-risk next move after the depth transition already paid off.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The short-run optimizer schedule is already doing the heavy lifting, so the next clean gain is a modest capacity bump: widen the second stage and give the classifier a bit more room. This keeps the script simple and avoids touching the training loop again.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Rollback the width bump that regressed the score and restore the previously best-visible channel multiplier and head size. This is the lowest-risk move because it returns the model to the last configuration that actually improved validation loss.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 4","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"With the best width/schedule settings restored, the next reasonable capacity move is to deepen both residual stages by one block each. This keeps the same optimization recipe and only increases representational depth.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Add a small amount of label smoothing to regularize the now-deeper model without changing runtime or the optimizer schedule. This is a low-risk way to try to improve validation cross-entropy after the architecture gains have mostly saturated.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"Nudge the OneCycle peak lower so the deeper, lightly regularized model can spend more steps refining instead of jumping around late in training. This is a cheap scalar tune with no runtime cost.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Rollback the label smoothing that regressed validation loss and restore the plain hard-label cross-entropy objective. This reverts to the better-performing loss setup with no runtime impact.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Restore the small label-smoothing regularizer that previously produced the best visible loss. The plain hard-label loss regressed, so this is the most direct recovery move.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"Restore the higher OneCycle peak that matched the best visible run; the lower peak looks underpowered for this depth/width setting. Keep the label smoothing in place and just return to the better LR scale.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Keep the best-known optimizer and regularization settings, and probe a slightly wider second stage now that the deeper backbone is working. This is the next cheapest capacity test after recovering the best loss setting.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The widened stage helped, so the cheapest remaining capacity bump is to give the classifier a bit more room. This keeps the trained feature extractor unchanged and should be low risk on runtime.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Rollback the wider classifier head that regressed validation loss and restore the better-known smaller head. This is the safest way to return to the previous best configuration.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The model is already regularized by label smoothing, so a slightly lower AdamW decay may help it fit a bit more cleanly without changing the schedule or architecture. This is a low-cost fine-tuning move near the current best point.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The current setup is already lightly regularized, and the loss is still inching down, so a slightly smaller AdamW decay is the safest final fine-tune to test. It keeps the best architecture and schedule intact.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Rollback the smaller weight decay that nudged validation loss upward and return to the prior best regularization setting. This is the safest final recovery move with the remaining budget.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The strongest remaining lever is one more residual block per stage; depth has been the most reliable source of gains so far.
